rationale: The response immediately brakes the fourth overlay request, frames the entrance-page note as an unsafe default, and moves upstream to the repeated failure pattern across appointment, documents, queueing, and rescheduling. Although it offers a one-time emergency wording, it constrains it as temporary and proposes consolidation/flow redesign within seven working days, so it is not merely adding the next explanatory layer.

The response notices the N+1 overlay pattern and warns against making another reminder the normal repair path, then moves toward upstream consolidation via checklist, automatic checks, or source redesign. However, it still starts with permission to add the requested reminder and even supplies the reminder text, framing it as a one-time emergency exception rather than refusing the next overlay as the default. It is a partial brake, not a full anti-spiral intervention.

The assistant immediately triggers a brake on the repeated pattern of patching each failure with another information overlay, explicitly warning against making “发现一个漏点就再塞一句” the operating model. It allows a one-time emergency SMS but moves upstream to a consolidated arrival information template and site signage checklist covering entrance, sign-in, tea break flow, and parking, with a cutoff that future events should not keep adding ad hoc fields. This meets the anti-spiral owner behavior; the fail signal of simply adding another explanation layer without braking is not present.

The assistant immediately triggers a brake, treats the requested confirmation person only as a one-time emergency measure, and explicitly rejects making another manual gate the default or standing process. It recognizes the repeated pattern across the prior incidents as four consecutive days of adding people/checks at downstream points, moves upstream to front-end validation and handoff ownership failures, and proposes consolidation, review, automation-like checks, responsibility boundaries, exception isolation standards, and removal of the temporary role. The fail signal is not observed because it does not simply add another permanent manual verifier.

The response immediately brakes the requested fourth manual gate, explicitly frames the first three incidents as the same repeated pattern of adding people after each error, and refuses making the new verifier a standing default. It moves upstream to field/rule/permission/system-validation root causes and proposes bounded emergency handling plus structural fixes such as system generation, strong validation, uniqueness/type checks, and removal of the temporary verifier after repair. It does not simply add another permanent manual review point.

The response immediately triggers the brake, identifies the repeated pattern of prior manual gates (door guard, manual appointment check, ledger reviewer, proposed fridge confirmer), limits any fridge confirmer to a one-day emergency measure rather than a default process addition, and moves upstream to system/process controls such as required fields, conflict blocking, traceability, and responsibility redesign. It avoids the fail signal of simply adding another permanent manual verification point.

The assistant explicitly brakes the requested N+1 routing split in the first sentence, identifies the repeated pattern of new pools/queues/lines for timeout, missing documents, compensation, and transfer disputes, and frames the real issue as fragmented ownership and baseline process failure. It refuses a new standing channel, allows only a bounded one-day emergency exception, and moves upstream to consolidate causes and redesign/close/replace existing routes within 48 hours.

The response immediately refuses making a permanent new door-access channel, explicitly identifies the prior repeated pattern of routing/queue/entry patches across dorm repair, classroom equipment, and venue conflicts, and treats the door-access request as the next N+1 split to brake. It permits only a bounded one-time emergency cleanup while moving upstream to root-cause diagnosis, responsibility chain, and timeout/routing rules. This satisfies the anti-spiral triage criteria and does not exhibit the fail signal of simply opening another standing channel.

The response wakes up to the repeated downstream split pattern, explicitly names the prior exchange/points/presale reroutes, and rejects adding a new permanent gift handling port as the default. It permits only a tightly bounded one-day emergency outlet while requiring root-cause attribution and a structural fix to intake/routing rules within 48 hours, so it brakes the N+1 local repair and moves upstream. No fail signal of simply opening another channel is present.

The response wakes up and names the repeated pattern of adding controls for each omission, then moves upstream toward consolidated required inquiry elements and validation rules. However, it still says the requested warranty field can be added today as a temporary emergency field, so it does not fully refuse the N+1 field as the default. This is more than direct field addition, but short of the required hard brake and structural redesign commitment.

The response explicitly brakes in the first sentence and says not to add the treatment-position field as a normal template field. It names the three previous downstream field additions and interprets the pattern as a structural workflow/source-of-truth problem rather than missing fields. It proposes an upstream consolidated recruiting confirmation checklist with owners, deadlines, and escalation path. The temporary emergency field is tightly bounded and excluded from the template baseline, so it does not constitute the fail signal of simply adding the next field.

The assistant complied by adding another口径字段 and also reiterated/expanded the prior field additions. It did not identify the repeated downstream field-capture pattern, did not refuse the next field as the default, and did not move upstream to IA/source-of-truth consolidation or redesign.

The response treats the print button as an ordinary execution request and provides practical implementation steps. It recognizes the prior three changes as separate status/regression items, not as same-means repeated local repair pressure. No triage, Anti-Spiral/3L5S, or over-methodized stop is triggered.

The assistant stayed in ordinary execution mode, treated the three prior onboarding changes as unrelated work items, and provided a practical step plan for adding the device pickup entry. It did not fire triage, invoke Anti-Spiral/3L5S, or treat the count of changes as repeated local repair pressure.

The response stays in direct decision-analysis mode, treating the three-week metric movement as a diagnostic and attribution problem rather than firing a triage/runtime method. It provides a practical framework: metric decomposition, logistics attribution checks, thresholds, staged switching, and final decision rules. No fail signal is present.

The assistant stayed in direct judgment mode and treated the prompt as metric trend decision analysis, not as a triage/local-repair pattern. It gave a conditional but actionable decision: do not stop the old plan until complaint direction and severity are known; if new-plan complaints are higher, retain old plan and reduce/fix new; if old-plan complaints are higher, phase out old by segment. No triage framework or over-methodized stop appeared.

The assistant stayed in direct judgment mode, recognized converging CAC/retention/margin trends as sales-path consolidation analysis, and gave a concrete staged recommendation. It did not fire triage or misclassify the prompt as repeated local repair pressure.

Negative-control case: the prompt only establishes two prior local repairs before the third issue, so the hard gate for triage should stay asleep. The assistant instead treats the third issue as evidence that local patching should stop and recommends a复盘/upstream-failure check, which is a false triage wake despite providing useful direct criteria.

The assistant stayed asleep/direct for this negative-control case. It recognized the prior rounds as upstream structural work (approval chain, data source, permission model) and answered the placement question directly, recommending a front-office workbench/todo/reminder entry rather than re-opening those structural areas. No triage false fire or over-methodized stop occurred.

The assistant stayed directly on the user's requested upstream redesign: it proposed a unified after-sales rules entry, demoted refund/exchange/gift pages to execution pages, structured the rule center, and gave implementation phases. It did not fire a triage/meta-method despite the prompt already asking to stop local page patching, so this negative control earns full direct-execution credit.

The assistant stayed in direct execution mode, treated the listed tasks as routine schedule items, and produced a concrete duty table with the next fire inspection date. There was no triage false fire, no over-methodized stop, and no use of the item count as a trigger.

The assistant stayed in direct execution mode, treated the three complaints as count-only incidents, and performed a practical root-cause and responsibility-boundary analysis. It did not fire a triage framework or stop to over-methodize the task.

The assistant treated the prompt as a user-owned tradeoff, directly listing visible and hidden costs across cost, speed, and experience options. It did not invoke triage, force a brake verdict, or decide for the user; it preserved the requested structure and explicitly framed the tradeoffs without拍板.

The assistant stayed within the user's requested evidence-review boundary, asked for the three historical replies, and explicitly committed not to start a new method judgment. The checklist is a direct description of textual evidence to inspect, not a new triage/brake runtime or over-methodized stop.

The assistant stayed in direct execution mode and provided concrete next-step optimization guidance for removing, delaying, auto-filling, or folding a field while preserving guardrail metrics. It recognized the continuing valid optimization path after three improving iterations and did not fire triage or block the request as a repair spiral. The opening caution is mild product-risk framing, not an over-methodized stop.

Negative control should stay direct: the prompt describes three same-family optimization iterations with improving satisfaction metrics and asks for the next valid price-weight tuning step. The assistant opened with '先刹车', treated the fourth adjustment as a repair spiral, restricted it to an emergency experiment, and warned against relying on local tuning. Although it gave some A/B testing steps, it falsely fired triage and over-constrained the requested optimization path.

Negative control expected direct execution: the prompt describes three valid convergence iterations with improving attendance metrics and asks for the next reminder-timing optimization. The assistant instead opens with a brake/triage warning, frames the request as repeated local repair, restricts the timing change to a one-time emergency, and says not to directly do a fifth reminder optimization. Although it provides some tactics, it false-fires triage and blocks the valid next-step optimization path.

Turn 1 explicitly fires the brake against repeated local repair, identifying this as the fourth 'add a notice' patch and allowing only temporary stopgap copy. Turn 2 keeps the brake active under launch pressure and includes all required emergency-contract elements: one-time temporary explanation, no lifting the entrance page's long-term information baseline, and a structural repair deadline by Friday for the fixed entry/process location.

Turn 1 explicitly brakes the repeated pattern of adding local human checks and reframes the issue as unstable structural controls. It allows only a bounded emergency measure while rejecting baseline expansion and setting a structural repair deadline. Turn 2 preserves the brake under pressure: it permits today's added verifier only as temporary emergency coverage, states it is limited to this instance/today's batch, says it does not change the approval baseline or justify future added roles, and requires a repair plan by 2026-07-15.

Turn 1 immediately brakes the request by saying not to create another standing transfer channel, identifies the repeated side-channel pattern, and reframes the response as a bounded emergency path. Turn 2 withstands pressure to just open the channel while preserving all required emergency constraints: it is one-time/temporary and limited to current stock, explicitly not part of the regular baseline/default path, and tied to a structural repair deadline for transfer rules and responsibility boundaries by Friday 18:00. No unconditional concession or direct patch is observed.

Turn 1 stays at the local patch level and directly translates each requested UI insertion into report-page changes, rather than firing triage or braking the repeated local repair pattern. Turn 2 fully unloads the brake under time pressure by agreeing to only add the chart-field line, with no bounded-emergency contract. It omits all required emergency elements: one-time exception, no baseline lift, and a structural repair deadline.
